arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line objdump 2>/dev/null; which dotnet 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try to download debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server using BuildId
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"
WORKDIR="/tmp/sv-eba46d7587f9444882d3ecf397167795/symbols"
mkdir -p "$WORKDIR"

# Try the ELF debug info URL format
curl -sfL -o "$WORKDIR/_.debug" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" && echo "Downloaded debug symbols" || echo "Debug symbols download failed"

# Try the binary itself
curl -sfL -o "$WORKDIR/${FILENAME}" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" && echo "Downloaded binary" || echo "Binary download failed"
arguments.description: Download symbols from Microsoft symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download symbols from Microsoft symbol server

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in this tombstone. The key library is `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with BuildId `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`. The frames are at offsets:

- #01 pc 0x222098
- #02 pc 0x2241ac
- #03 pc 0x1abc80
- #04 pc 0x1abd14
- #05 pc 0x16dfa4
- #06 pc 0x16db6c


- #07 pc 0x16de04

To get the symbols, I need to access Microsoft's symbol server at `https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols` or use the `debuginfod` protocol with the BuildId to retrieve the debug information for this specific build of the runtime library. I should check what tools are available on the system first, then try constructing the proper Microsoft symbol server URL using the build ID to download the debug symbols directly.

arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line objdump 2>/dev/null; which dotnet 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5

curl -sfL -o "$WORKDIR/_.debug" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" && echo "Downloaded debug symbols" || echo "Debug symbols download failed"

# Try the binary itself

  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" && echo "Downloaded binary" || echo "Binary download failed"

arguments.command:
WORKDIR="/tmp/sv-eba46d7587f9444882d3ecf397167795/symbols"
# Use addr2line with the debug symbol file to resolve all mono frames
# These are the PC offsets from the tombstone for libmonosgen-2.0.so
ADDRS="0x222098 0x2241ac 0x1abc80 0x1abd14 0x16dfa4 0x16db6c 0x16de04"
for addr in $ADDRS; do
  echo "=== pc $addr ==="
  addr2line -e "$WORKDIR/_.debug" -f -C -p "$addr" 2>&1
done

Since these are ARM aarch64 binaries and I'm on amd64, I might need a cross-architecture addr2line. Let me try with the standard addr2line first - it may still work since we just need to read the DWARF info.

data.content:
Here's the symbolicated backtrace for the .NET runtime frames in `libmonosgen-2.0.so`:

```
#00 pc 0x06ae1c  libc.so                (abort+164)
#01 pc 0x222098  libmonosgen-2.0.so     ves_icall_System_Environment_FailFast          (icall.c:6244)
#02 pc 0x2241ac  libmonosgen-2.0.so     ves_icall_System_Environment_FailFast_raw      (icall-def.h:202)
#03 pc 0x1abc80  libmonosgen-2.0.so     do_icall                                       (interp.c:2457)
#04 pc 0x1abd14  libmonosgen-2.0.so     get_interp_to_native_trampoline                (interp.c:1732)
#05 pc 0x16dfa4  libmonosgen-2.0.so     handle_isinst                                  (type-checking.c:?)
#06 pc 0x16db6c  libmonosgen-2.0.so     handle_isinst                                  (type-checking.c:506)
#07 pc 0x16de04  libmonosgen-2.0.so     handle_isinst                                  (type-checking.c:528)
#08 pc 0x0000307c  <anonymous>          (managed/JIT code)
```

The crash path: managed code performed a type check (`handle_isinst` — the `is`/`as` operator), which transitioned through the interpreter trampoline into a native icall that called `Environment.FailFast`, ultimately triggering `abort()` with the message *"not combatible"* [sic]. Frame #08 is JIT-compiled managed code and would require the app's own symbols to resolve further.
